programs:
Programs
Province-Wide Human Resource Information System (HRIS) Deployment - develop and configure an interoperable HRIS covering all departments and at least 50% component LGUs

Electronic Document Management System (eDMS) Implementation - develop and operationalize a province-wide eDMS

Citizen's Charter Review and Organizational Development Plan Updating - conduct a systematic review of all provincial government offices' Citizen's Charter aligned with ARTA

Policies
Provincial Government Process Re-engineering Policy (Lean Government Policy) - adopt and institutionalize to direct all provincia departments and component LGUs

Provincial HRIS and eDMS Mandatory Use Policy - issue an executiveo order mandating the adoption of HRIS and eDMS across departments

Programs
Provincial Integrated Real-Time Governance Dashboard Deployment - develop, deploy, and operationalize a provincial real-time governance dashboard that aggregates and visualizes live data streams from the HRIS, eDMS, and at least three sectoral databases — including health, agriculture, and social welfare

Provincial Digital Public Feedback and Complaints Management System Implementation -develop and deploy a multi-channel digital platform for receiving, tracking, routing, and resolving public feedback and complaints

Capacity Building Program on Data Analytics, Visualization, and Strategic Planning for Government Personnel - design and implement a structured, multi-batch capacity building program for provincial and component LGU government staff covering core competencies in data analytics, data visualization tools, and strategic planning methodologies

Policies
Provincial Open Skills Fair and Competency Development Policy - institutionalize an annual Provincial Open Skills Fair as a mandatory competency development and career mobility mechanism for all provincial government and component LGU personnel

Provincial Performance-based Advancement and Mentorship Policy - adoption and institutionalization to direct all provincial departments and component LGUs to link promotion and the like to  verified Strategic Performance Management System (SPMS)

Provincial Data Governance Framework Policy - adopt to establish binding standards for datta collection, interoperability, and security across all departments, and LGUs

Programs
Unified Digital Services Portal - deploy, develop and single , unified services portal integrating all public services across departments

Cybersecurity Plan Implementation and Digital Infrastructure Protection Program - develop, adopt, and operationalize a comprehensive Cybersecurity Plan governing the security architecture of all interconnected provincial digital systems

Policies
Government Interoperability and Open Standards Policy - EO mandating that all information systems must comply with open, standardized APIs and data exchance protocols (refer to DICT's PeGIF)

Programs
AI-Powered Predictive Governance and Human Resource Forecasting System Development - develop and deploy an AI-powered predictive analytics system integrated with the existing HRIS, governance dashboard, and sectoral databases to generate data-driven forecasts

IoT Sensor Network Deployment for Smart Governance Monitoring - procurement, installation, and operationalization of and IoT sensor network across urban and rural areas to enable real-time monitoring of key governance and service delivery parameters

Long-Term AI Literacy and Professional Development Program for Government Personnel Institutionalization - design, adopt, and institutionalize a multi-year AI literacy and professional development program for government employees and LGU personnel

Policies
Smart Governance and Responsible Technology Adoption Policy - policy to institutionalize the use of emerging technologies in the delivery of public service

Full Province-Wide HRIS and eDMS Sustainment and Continuous Improvement Program

Integrated Smart Governance Platform Sustainment and Innovation Program

AI Literacy and Professional Development Program — Advanced and Sustaining Cycle

Institutional Capacity Maturity Assessment and Recognition Program

subsectors.name: Fiscal Management
subsectors.description: Maximized resource mobilization and utilization through enhanced financial management systems (for real-time reporting, ensured fiscal transparency and accountability, for the efficient delivery of public services.)

Fiscal transparency, participatory monitoring, and full tax declaration digitalization achieved through a public portal and modernized financial systems.
Automated financial controls institutionalized within the IFMS, enabling real-time validation, monitoring, and system-enforced compliance.
Data-driven resource mobilization institutionalized through predictive analytics, with significantly reduced audit findings reflecting mature fiscal discipline.

Integrated Financial Management System (IFMS) Deployment - Procure, configure, and deploy a centralized IFMS covering 100% of provincial government departments and at least 50% of component LGUs

IFMS Capacity Building and Personnel Retooling Program for Fiscal and Accounting Personnel - Design and implement a structured, multi-batch training and retooling program covering 100% of fiscal, accounting, budget, and treasury personnel at the provincial level and at least 70% of component LGU fiscal officers

One-Day Bidding Caravan Program

IFMS Mandatory Adoption and Financial Data Harmonization Policy

Transparency Seal and Full Disclosure Policy Compliance Ordinance

Public Accountability Assembly Program - Conduct an annual Provincial Public Accountability Assembly

Project Public Notice Board Program - Install standardized Project Public Notice Boards at all active provincially funded infrastructure and development project sites

Digital Twin and Drone-Based Mapping Program for Fiscal and Infrastructure Planning - Conduct a province-wide drone-assisted aerial mapping and digital twin development program

Mobile Application for Taxpayer Access and Budget Transparency Development and Deployment - Develop and deploy a Provincial Mobile Application providing citizens with real-time access to tax-related services

Digital Tax Declaration Data-Banking Program Phase 2 (100% Completion)

Citizen Procurement Watchdog Policy 

Fiscal Transparency and Open Data Policy

Full Disclosure Policy LED Display and Physical Posting Program Policy - Issue a Provincial Executive Order directing the installation and maintenance of LED display boards at strategic public locations

Full End-to-End Digital Procurement System Implementation - Complete the full digitization of the entire procurement cycle

Digital Fiscal Risk Management Framework Development and Operationalization - Develop and operationalize a Digital Fiscal Risk Management Framework

Digital Asset Inventory and Management System Full Deployment - Complete the development and full deployment of a comprehensive Provincial Digital Asset Inventory and Management System

subsectors.name: Legislative Output

programs:
Programs
Provincial Governance Assessment and ISO Readiness Framework Establishment - Establish a formal Provincial Governance Assessment and ISO Readiness Framework thru an EO on the creation of a Provincial Governance Assessment Team (PGAT) composed of representatives from the PPDO, HRMO, MISO, and the Sangguniang Panlalawigan Secretariat

Province-Wide Governance Assessment and ISO Readiness Data Gathering Program - Conduct a comprehensive province-wide governance assessment and ISO readiness data gathering program covering 100% of PLGU departments and all 21 component LGUs

Resolution Adopting the ISO Governance and Digital Transformation Act of Surigao del Norte - Enact a Sangguniang Panlalawigan Resolution, supported by a Provincial Executive Order on the adoption of the ISO Governance and Digital Transformation Act of Surigao del Norte, which mandates the phased adoption of ISO 9001:2015 (Quality Management System) and ISO/IEC 27001:2022 (Information Security Management System)

Programs
Phased ISO 9001:2015 Certification Implementation and LGU Cluster Management Program - Implement the first phase of the Provincial ISO Adoption Master Plan through a cluster-based approach

Provincial ISO Internal Audit Capacity Building and Certification Management Program - Design and implement a structured Internal Audit Capacity Building Program — training at least one designated Internal Quality Auditor (IQA) per PLGU department and component LGU in ISO 9001:2015 internal audit methodology, non-conformity identification, corrective action planning, and management review facilitation

Policies
Resolution Amending the ISO Governance and Digital Transformation Act — Phased Development and Cluster Management Framework - Amend the 2028 Sangguniang Panlalawigan Resolution adopting the ISO Governance and Digital Transformation Act to incorporate a formal Phased Development and Cluster Management Framework

Programs
ISO 9001:2015 Certification Expansion and ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Phased Certification Rollout Program
Expand ISO 9001:2015 certification to at least 70% of component LGUs and 100% of PLGU departments by end of 2034 and simultaneously initiating the phased rollout of ISO/IEC 27001:2022 (Information Security Management System) certification

Provincial Digital Infrastructure Upgrading Program for ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Compliance - Conduct a comprehensive assessment and systematic upgrading of the digital infrastructure of all PLGU departments and component LGUs identified for ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification

Provincial ISO Compliance Monitoring and Catch-Up Plan Implementation Program - Establish and operationalize a centralized ISO Compliance Dashboard (integrated with the provincial governance monitoring system)

Policies
Resolution Establishing the Provincial ISO Compliance Dashboard and Mandatory Compliance Reporting Policy - Enact a Sangguniang Panlalawigan Resolution mandating the establishment and continuous operation of a centralized Provincial ISO Compliance Dashboard

Resolution Mandating Information Security Prioritization and ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Fast-Track Certification for Sensitive Data-Handling Offices - Enact a Sangguniang Panlalawigan Resolution directing the prioritization of ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification for all provincial government offices handling sensitive personal, financial, health, and assessment data

Programs
Last Mile ISO 9001:2015 Certification Initiative - Design and implement a targeted Last Mile Certification Program to achieve 100% ISO 9001:2015 certification coverage across all remaining uncertified component LGUs

Resolution Adopting ISO/IEC 27701:2019 (Privacy Information Management System) as a Supplementary Standard to ISO/IEC 27001:2022

Programs
Integrated ISO Management System Sustainment Program (ISO 9001:2015, ISO/IEC 27001:2022, and ISO/IEC 27701:2019)
Sustain and continuously improve the three-standard integrated ISO management system — covering Quality Management (ISO 9001:2015), Information Security Management (ISO/IEC 27001:2022), and Privacy Information Management (ISO/IEC 27701:2019) 

Provincial ISO Compliance Dashboard Sustainment and Integrated Governance Monitoring Program
Sustain and continuously upgrade the centralized Provincial ISO Compliance Dashboard

Provincial ISO Governance Excellence Recognition and National Benchmarking Program

Provincial ISO Knowledge Management and Inter-LGU Learning Exchange Program
Establish and operationalize a Provincial ISO Knowledge Management Program that documents, packages, and disseminates the province's ISO adoption experience, best practices, and governance innovations through structured knowledge products and at least two annual inter-LGU learning exchange events

Resolution Amending the ISO Governance and Digital Transformation Act

Resolution Integrating ISO Governance Performance into the Provincial Legislative Agenda and Annual State of the Province Address

programs:
Programs
Cooperative Compliance and Governance Strengthening Program
Conduct Compliance Clinics with CDA and LGUs, develop and distribute a Coop Governance Toolkit (digital templates and manuals), and create online reporting portals for easier CDA compliance submission across all registered cooperatives province-wide. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead implementing office in coordination with component LGU cooperative focal persons.

Cooperative Leadership and Governance Training Program
Launch a Leadership and Governance Training Series for cooperative officers and organize regional orientation seminars for newly registered cooperatives, covering governance, financial management, and statutory compliance standards. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with PPDO.

Policies
Mandatory Compliance Orientation Policy
Require all newly registered and existing cooperatives to complete a CDA-prescribed compliance orientation covering governance, financial reporting, and statutory requirements as a condition for provincial cooperative program participation and incentive eligibility.

Digital Reporting Policy
Mandate all cooperatives receiving provincial support to submit compliance and financial reports through the provincial online reporting portal, with the CDA Liaison Office serving as system administrator and compliance monitor.

Programs
Cooperative Financing and Capitalization Program
Implement low-interest loan and matching grant programs for cooperatives in coordination with LandBank, DBP, and CDA, and establish Regional Cooperative Credit Bureaus to strengthen member financial accountability and creditworthiness. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with PBO and PPDO.

Financial Literacy and Enterprise Development Training Program
Conduct province-wide Financial Literacy Campaigns for cooperative members and leaders, and roll out Enterprise Development Training covering marketing, bookkeeping, and cost control targeting at least 70% of registered cooperatives by end of 2031. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with TESDA-SDN and DTI-SDN.

Cooperative Insurance and Guarantee Scheme Pilot Program
Pilot Coop Insurance and Guarantee Schemes in priority cooperatives to reduce lending risks and expand access to formal credit, in coordination with PCIC, PhilGuarantee, and accredited insurance providers. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office.

Policies
Coop Credit Access Policy
Direct the provincial government to facilitate and co-guarantee cooperative access to formal credit through LandBank, DBP, CDA revolving funds, and microfinance institutions, with the PBO and CDA Liaison Office jointly monitoring credit program utilization and repayment performance. 

Enterprise Development Policy
Mandate all cooperatives receiving provincial financial support to submit and implement Enterprise Development Plans covering marketing strategy, cost efficiency measures, and revenue diversification within 12 months of fund release, with the CDA Liaison Office monitoring compliance.

Programs
Coop Trade Hubs and Annual Cooperative Expo Program
Launch provincial Coop Trade Hubs as permanent market access facilities for cooperative products and services, and organize an Annual Cooperative Expo showcasing provincial cooperative products to local, regional, and national buyers. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with DTI-SDN and component LGUs.

Digital Marketing, E-Commerce, and Coop-to-Coop Networking Program
Support Digital Marketing and E-Commerce Adoption for cooperatives through platform training and digital marketplace enrollment, and facilitate Coop-to-Coop Networking Activities forming supply and value chain clusters across municipalities. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with DICT-SDN and DTI-SDN.

Export Readiness Training Program
Provide Export Readiness Training for cooperatives with identified export potential, covering product standards, packaging, export documentation, and international market access pathways in coordination with DA Caraga, BFAR Caraga, and DTI-SDN. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office.

Policies
Inclusive Procurement Policy
Require provincial government departments and component LGUs to prioritize cooperative-produced goods and services in government procurement processes compliant with RA 9184, with the BAC Secretariat and CDA Liaison Office jointly monitoring cooperative procurement participation rates. 

Digital Market Access Policy
Mandate all cooperatives receiving provincial trade support to register on at least one provincial or national digital marketplace platform, with the MISO and CDA Liaison Office providing technical onboarding assistance and monitoring digital sales performance.

Programs
Coop Research and Development Centers Program
Establish Coop R&D Centers in partnership with SUCs — particularly SNSU and Caraga State University — supporting applied research on cooperative business models, green technologies, and market innovations relevant to Surigao del Norte's priority sectors. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with CHED Caraga and partner SUCs.

Coop Innovation and Technology Grants Program
Roll out Coop Innovation and Technology Grants funding cooperative adoption of automation, renewable energy, AI tools, and blockchain-based supply chain transparency, targeting financial self-sustainability for at least 75% of registered cooperatives by end of 2037. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with DOST-SDN and DTI-SDN.

Industry 4.0 Skills Training and Green Cooperative Program
Train cooperative members in Industry 4.0 competencies (digital tools, e-commerce, data analytics) and implement the Green Cooperative Program supporting eco-tourism, organic farming, and clean energy cooperatives, with training delivered through the Coop R&D Centers in partnership with SUCs. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with TESDA-SDN and PENRO.

Cooperative Innovation Summit Program
Organize annual Cooperative Innovation Summits for provincial and regional knowledge sharing on cooperative best practices, technology adoption, and green business innovations, positioning Surigao del Norte cooperatives as regional models for sustainable enterprise development. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with CDA Regional Office and PPDO.

Policies
Innovation Incentive Policy
Provide provincial fiscal incentives — including local tax reductions and priority access to provincial grants — for cooperatives adopting certified green business practices, Industry 4.0 technologies, or renewable energy systems, with the CDA Liaison Office and PTO jointly administering the incentive program.

Coop–SUC Partnership Policy
Mandate the CDA Liaison Office to formalize MOAs between provincial cooperatives and at least two SUCs for R&D collaboration, technology transfer, student practicum hosting, and joint enterprise development, with partnerships reviewed and renewed every three years.

Programs
National Cooperative Enterprise Network and Global Trade Linkages Program
Form the National Cooperative Enterprise Network (NCEN) for provincial cooperative branding and global trade, and develop a Global Trade Linkages Program facilitating cooperative exports, joint ventures, and international partnerships

Coop Leaders Academy and Succession Planning Program
Institutionalize the Coop Leaders Academy providing advanced governance training and succession planning for cooperative officers, ensuring leadership continuity and institutional resilience across all generations of cooperative membership

Cooperative Member Social Protection Scheme Program
Launch comprehensive Social Protection Schemes for cooperative members

Public-Private-People Partnership (4Ps) Inclusive Community Development Sustainment Program
Sustain Public-Private-People Partnership projects linking cooperatives, LGUs, private enterprises, and community organizations for inclusive community development

Policies
Global Market Integration Policy
Direct the provincial government to actively facilitate provincial cooperative access to national and international markets

Social Protection Policy
Mandate all cooperatives with 50 or more active members to enroll members in at least one PhilHealth, SSS, or accredited insurance program as a condition for continued provincial incentive eligibility

Public-Private-People Partnership Policy (4Ps)
Institutionalize 4Ps as a permanent provincial cooperative development strategy
